```
# synix

> A build system for agent memory

## What it does

Synix transforms raw conversation data into hierarchical, searchable memory with full provenance tracking. Declare your memory architecture in Python, build it incrementally, then change it — only affected layers rebuild. Think `make` or `dbt`, but for AI agent memory.

## Key concepts

- **Pipeline** — Python definition of your memory architecture (sources, transforms, projections)
- **Layer** — typed processing step in the build DAG (Source, Transform, SearchIndex, FlatFile)
- **Artifact** — immutable, versioned build output with content-addressed identity (SHA256)
- **Transform** — LLM-powered processing step that converts inputs to outputs
- **Projection** — materialized output (search index or flat file) for agent consumption
- **Provenance** — every artifact traces back through dependency graph to source conversations
- **Fingerprint** — build cache key capturing inputs, prompts, model config, and transform code

## Installation and quick start

```bash
# Install and initialize
uvx synix init my-project
cd my-project

# Add API key and source files, then build
uvx synix build

# Search and explore
uvx synix search "hiking"
uvx synix list
uvx synix show final-report
```

## CLI commands

- `uvx synix init <name>` — scaffold new project with pipeline and sources
- `uvx synix build` — run pipeline, rebuild only what changed
- `uvx synix plan` — dry-run showing what would build and estimated cost
- `uvx synix search <query>` — full-text/semantic search with provenance
- `uvx synix list [layer]` — show all artifacts, optionally filtered
- `uvx synix show <id>` — display artifact content
- `uvx synix lineage <id>` — show provenance chain
- `uvx synix batch-build run` — submit via OpenAI Batch API for 50% cost savings
- `uvx synix validate` — run declared validators on build artifacts
- `uvx synix clean` — delete build directory

## Architecture overview

```
src/synix/
├── core/models.py          # Pipeline, Layer, Transform base classes
├── build/
│   ├── runner.py           # Execute pipeline, walk DAG, run transforms
│   ├── artifacts.py        # Artifact storage, content-addressed cache
│   ├── fingerprint.py      # Build fingerprints for cache invalidation
│   ├── llm_transforms.py   # Built-in transforms (EpisodeSummary, etc.)
│   ├── validators.py       # Validation framework
│   └── batch.py           # OpenAI Batch API support
├── search/
│   ├── indexer.py          # SQLite FTS5 + semantic indexing
│   ├── retriever.py        # Hybrid search with RRF fusion
│   └── embeddings.py       # Embedding providers (OpenAI, fastembed)
├── transforms/             # Re-exports: EpisodeSummary, MonthlyRollup, etc.
├── validators/             # Re-exports: PII, Citation, SemanticConflict
└── cli/                   # Click commands with Rich formatting
```

## Pipeline definition

Python-first pipeline definitions with real object references:

```python
from synix import Pipeline, Source, SearchIndex
from synix.transforms import EpisodeSummary, MonthlyRollup, CoreSynthesis

pipeline = Pipeline("my-pipeline")
pipeline.llm_config = {"provider": "anthropic", "model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"}

# Define layers
transcripts = Source("transcripts", source_dir="./sources")
episodes = EpisodeSummary("episodes", depends_on=[transcripts])
monthly = MonthlyRollup("monthly", depends_on=[episodes])
core = CoreSynthesis("core", depends_on=[monthly])

# Add to pipeline
pipeline.add(transcripts, episodes, monthly, core)
pipeline.add(SearchIndex("search", sources=[episodes, monthly, core]))
```

## Important constraints

- **SQLite only** — no external databases required, filesystem + SQLite FTS5
- **Local execution** — no web UI, CLI-first tool
- **Content-addressed caching** — artifacts identified by SHA256 of content
- **Python 3.11+ required** — uses modern Python features
- **LLM providers** — OpenAI and Anthropic native, others via base_url override
- **Fingerprint-based rebuilds** — change prompt/model/config → automatic cache invalidation
- **Experimental features** — batch-build and validate/fix workflows may change
```
